title: Crunchy Crumbly Portuguese Cod Skillet
description: This dish features cod cooked in a crumbly mixture of breadcrumbs and spices, served with a side of sauteed vegetables and a drizzle of fish sauce, making for a satisfying midnight craving snack. It is prepared in a way that is friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs, salt, and black pepper.
Dip each cod fillet into the breadcrumb mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the chopped bell pepper and cook until it is tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the coated cod fillets to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and drizzle the fish sauce over the top of the cod fillets.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the dish and serve hot.
